rationale: In the theory section, the cited work is used to support the hedonic-versus-utilitarian product distinction, one of the paper's two central moderators of MDA effectiveness. In the methods, the authors explicitly report giving judges Dhar and Wertenbroch's (2000) definitions to classify campaign products, indicating direct operational use. This is therefore not a passing literature-list citation; it meaningfully informs both conceptual setup and variable construction.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) to define the hedonic-versus-utilitarian product dimension and uses those definitions in coding products across campaigns. It applies that framework to test which products are more responsive to mobile display advertising.
